title: AGI/ASI Governance Master Blueprint
subtitle: Institutional-grade governance for F500 / G2000 / G-SIFIs — enterprise, frontier, and civilizational scale (2026-2030)
owner: Chief AI Officer (CAIO) + Chief Risk Officer (CRO) + Board AI/Risk Committee
regimes: NIST AI RMF 1.0 + Generative AI Profile
GDPR / UK GDPR / CCPA / PDPA-SG / PDPO-HK
FSB AI in Finance + Compute Concentration
AISI UK + US AISI joint frameworks
GPAI Code of Practice + Hiroshima Process
Bletchley + Seoul + Paris AI Safety Summits

directive.format: Machine-parsable governance directive for AGI-grade enterprise AI

systems: All AI systems including agents, LLMs, predictive models, decisioning systems, frontier R&D

pillars.P1_Technical: Engineering controls, model lifecycle, deterministic replay, drift
pillars.P2_Ethical: Values alignment, fairness, fundamental rights, human dignity
pillars.P3_Legal: Regulatory compliance, contractual obligations, liability allocation
pillars.P4_Operational: Day-to-day operation, incident response, monitoring, SLAs
pillars.P5_Risk: Inherent/residual risk, RCSA, three lines of defence, capital allocation

decisionHierarchy: Tier-1 (customer-facing/material): CAIO + CRO dual approval; Board notification
Tier-2 (Annex IV high-risk/regulated): CAIO + CRO + GC + Board AI/Risk Committee approval
Tier-3 (frontier/dual-use): All Tier-2 + ExCo + CEO + AISI joint testing
Tier-4 (ASI candidate / capability gain): All Tier-3 + Board chair + supervisor pre-clearance + treaty body notification

escalation.Tier-1_incident: Model Owner -> CAIO within 1h; CRO + CISO within 4h
escalation.Tier-2_incident: Add GC within 4h; Board AI Cttee chair within 24h
escalation.Tier-3_incident: Add CEO within 4h; Board chair within 8h; regulator within 24-72h per regime
escalation.Tier-4_incident: Immediate containment (T4 air-gap); CEO + Board chair + AISI within 1h; treaty body within 24h

globalBodies: GACRLS (Global AI Compute Resource Licensing System)

modules.title: Regulatory Compliance Architectures (EU AI Act, NIST RMF, ISO 42001, GDPR, FCRA, Basel III, SR 11-7)
modules.summary: Cross-regime compliance reference architecture mapping each obligation to engineering controls, evidence artifacts, and auditor workflows for the 2026-2030 horizon.

EU_AI_Act: Article 9: Risk management system across lifecycle
Article 11 + Annex IV: Technical documentation pack
Article 50-52: Transparency for GPAI + foundation models
NIST_RMF: GOVERN: Establish AI risk culture, roles, accountability
Generative AI Profile: 12 risk categories + 200+ actions
ISO_42001: Clause 4: Context of organisation + interested parties
Clause 6: Planning + AI risk + AI impact assessment
Clause 8: Operation (lifecycle, third-party, controls Annex A)
Clause 9: Performance evaluation + internal audit + management review
Annex A (38 controls): policies, internal organization, resources, impact assessment, lifecycle, data, information for interested parties, AI system use, third-party relationships
GDPR_UK_GDPR: Art.5: Principles (lawfulness, fairness, purpose limitation, minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity, accountability)
Art.25: Data protection by design and by default
FCRA_ECOA_UDAAP: FCRA s.615(a): Adverse action notice with reasons
ECOA Reg B s.1002.9: Notice of action taken + reasons
ECOA s.1002.6: Rules concerning evaluation of applications
UDAAP: Avoid unfair, deceptive, abusive practices in AI-driven products
Basel_III_IV: IRB for internal ratings (PD, LGD, EAD)
AI-augmented models require independent validation under SR 11-7
SR_11_7_SR_13_19: Conceptual soundness + ongoing monitoring + outcomes analysis

obligationToControl: EU AI Act Art.9 -> RCSA workflow + RCM rows + Risk Register schema
EU AI Act Art.10 -> Lineage SCH (provenance) + consent OPA policy + curation pipeline
EU AI Act Art.11/Annex IV -> Annex IV pack template (Appendix A) + AISRG R-01..R-12
EU AI Act Art.12 -> Kafka WORM audit + PQC-signed events + Merkle anchoring
EU AI Act Art.13 -> Model Card v2 + GPAI summary + deployer pack
EU AI Act Art.14 -> Human-in-loop intervention API + override audit + training programme
EU AI Act Art.15 -> Robustness eval battery + adversarial red team + bug bounty
EU AI Act Art.27 -> FRIA template (Appendix B) with stakeholder consultation evidence
EU AI Act Art.55 -> Systemic risk eval + AISI joint testing + serious incident pipeline
NIST GOVERN -> AI Charter + RACI + Board attestation + culture survey
NIST MAP -> Use case registry + impact assessment + intended/foreseeable use
NIST MEASURE -> Eval batteries + KPIs + benchmarks + red team
NIST MANAGE -> Risk treatment plan + monitoring + comms + retrospectives
ISO 42001 Annex A -> Mapped 1:1 to OPA policy bundle (38 Rego packages)
GDPR Art.22 -> Human-review escalation + automated-decision register
GDPR Art.25 -> Privacy-by-design checklist (Appendix C) + DPIA template
GDPR Art.32 -> Encryption (PQC), pseudonymisation, access controls, BCP
FCRA s.615 -> Adverse Action Engine + SHAP/counterfactual reasons + appeal flow
ECOA Reg B -> Disparate impact monitor (K-07) + fair lending committee
Basel III -> Capital model validation + backtesting + replay (CODE-05 from WP-052)
SR 11-7 -> MRM tiering + independent validation + effective challenge documented

content.format: PDF/A-3 for narrative + JSON-LD for structured + PQC-signed manifest
content.retention: 10 years standard; 25 years for Tier-2+ (Annex IV high-risk) and Tier-3+ (frontier)
content.access: Role-based + zk-SNARK proof for regulator sandbox + auditor read-only

phases: Phase 1 — Pre-engagement: scope letter, NDA, system inventory snapshot
Phase 2 — Walkthrough: governance kernel demo, OPA policy library, WORM replay
Phase 3 — Testing: sample-based control testing (SCH-01..SCH-12), evidence pull from AISRG
Phase 4 — Independent validation: re-run replay harness on selected Tier-1 models
Phase 5 — Reporting: ISAE 3000 / SSAE 18 / AAF 01/20 attestation per scope
Phase 6 — Remediation tracking: management response register + closure attestation
content.sla: Initial engagement 8-12 weeks; annual recurrence 4-6 weeks

conflicts: GDPR erasure vs Annex IV WORM retention -> WORM exemption registry + cryptographic deletion of derived data
US discovery vs EU privacy -> Standard Contractual Clauses + data localisation + legal hold playbook
EU AI Act Art.50 transparency vs trade secret -> Tiered disclosure (regulator full, public summary)
MAS FEAT explainability vs IP -> Methodology disclosure without revealing weights
EO 14110 reporting vs EU AI Act systemic risk -> Single source of truth + dual filings
content.playbook: Conflicts logged in Conflict Register (Appendix D), reviewed monthly by GC + DPO + Treaty Liaison, escalated to Board AI Cttee quarterly

modules.title: Multilayered AI Governance Structures (Technical, Ethical, Legal, Operational, Risk)
modules.summary: Five-pillar governance taxonomy with roles, decision hierarchies, and incident escalation chains explicitly designed for AGI/ASI-grade systems.

content.P1_Technical: Engineering controls (lifecycle, replay, drift, security, telemetry), owned by CTO + CAIO
content.P2_Ethical: Values, fairness, fundamental rights, dignity, owned by Chief Ethics Officer + Ethics Board
content.P3_Legal: Regulatory compliance, contracts, liability, IP, owned by GC + DPO + Treaty Liaison
content.P4_Operational: BAU operations, incident response, SLAs, change management, owned by COO + Head of AI Ops
content.P5_Risk: Inherent/residual risk, 3LoD, capital, RCSA, owned by CRO + Head of MRM
content.intersection: All five pillars meet at the Board AI/Risk Committee with the CAIO as executive sponsor

executive: CAIO (Chief AI Officer) — executive accountability for all AI
Head of MRM — model risk under SR 11-7
operational: Head of Fair Lending / Consumer Outcomes
Head of AISRG (AI Safety Report Generator)

content.T0_low_risk_internal: Model Owner approval; quarterly batch review by MRM
content.T1_customer_facing_material: CAIO + CRO dual approval; Board notification within 30 days
content.T2_Annex_IV_high_risk_regulated: CAIO + CRO + GC + Board AI Cttee approval; supervisor notification per regime
content.T3_frontier_dual_use: Tier-2 quorum + ExCo + CEO + AISI joint testing pre-deploy; serious incident pipeline armed
content.T4_ASI_candidate_capability_gain: Tier-3 quorum + Board chair + supervisor pre-clearance + treaty body (ICGC/GFMCF) notification + air-gap deployment only
content.decisionLog: Every tier decision is WORM-logged (SCH-08) with PQC signature of approvers

content.detection: Sentinel v2.4 + GAI-SOC monitor 30+ signal streams (CRP, fairness, drift, security, capability)
triage_minutes: 0-15m: First responder triage; severity score (S1 critical / S2 major / S3 moderate / S4 minor)
15-60m: Containment action (rollback, throttle, isolate, T4 air-gap if Tier-3+)
regulator_clocks: EU AI Act serious incident: <= 15 days (Art.73)
PRA operational incident: 'as soon as possible'
SR 11-7 material model issue: per institutional policy (typically <= 30 days)
AISI joint frontier incident: per joint testing agreement (typically <= 24h)
post_incident: Lessons learned + control changes within 60 days
Public disclosure if material (per Consumer Duty / SEC / etc.)

sections.title: RACI Snapshot (5 pillars x key activities)

content.model_charter_approval: R: CAIO; A: Board AI Cttee; C: CRO/GC/DPO/CISO; I: ExCo
content.Annex_IV_pack_signoff: R: CAIO; A: Board AI Cttee chair; C: GC/CRO/DPO; I: Supervisors
content.tier1_model_deployment: R: Model Owner; A: CAIO+CRO; C: GC/CISO/MRM; I: Board AI Cttee
content.tier3_frontier_training_kickoff: R: AI Safety Lead; A: CEO+Board chair; C: AISI/Treaty Liaison; I: ICGC
content.tier4_capability_gain_response: R: AI Safety Lead+CISO; A: CEO+Board chair; C: GC/Treaty Liaison; I: GACMO/AISI
content.annual_governance_audit: R: Internal Audit; A: Board Audit Cttee; C: External auditor; I: Board

modules.title: Enterprise AI Reference Architectures + Trust/Compliance Stacks
modules.summary: Reference stack: Kafka ACL governance, continuous compliance with policy-as-code (OPA), Terraform/CI/CD repository patterns, WORM audit storage, automated verification, and auditor workflows.

planes: Data plane: ingestion -> feature store -> training -> registry -> serving
Governance plane: OPA + Kafka WORM + PQC-KMS + zk-SNARK verifier + AISRG
Observability plane: OpenTelemetry + Grafana + AI-specific dashboards (CRP/drift/fairness/carbon)
Security plane: Vault + IAM + Kafka ACL + admission webhooks + red-team CI
Coordination plane: Treaty Liaison API + global registry submitters + AISI handover
content.trustBoundary: Every cross-plane call is mediated by OPA + WORM logged + PQC signed

content.topology: Dedicated WORM cluster (kafka-worm:9093) + ops cluster + tenant clusters
acl_principles: Principal-of-least-privilege: producers ONLY to their owning topic
Auditor role: read-only on ALL topics
content.enforcement: Kafka SASL/SCRAM + mTLS + ACL CLI + IaC via Terraform Cloud

content.test_coverage: K-06 KPI: >= 95% Rego unit test coverage; conftest in CI
content.evaluation: Evaluated at (i) PR open, (ii) admission webhook, (iii) runtime sidecar, (iv) AISRG section build
content.distribution: OPA bundle server (signed bundles) + push to all sidecars within 60s

monorepo_layout: /iac/             Terraform modules (golden env, networking, KMS, Kafka)
/models/          per-model directory (card, training, eval, deploy spec)
ci_gates: Gate-1 (PR open): lint + conftest + policy unit tests + secret scan + SBOM-AI
Gate-2 (PR merge): full integration test + replay (sample) + fairness regression
Gate-3 (deploy staging): admission webhook + canary CRP monitor
Gate-4 (deploy prod): tier-appropriate approval chain + WORM event emit
Gate-5 (post-deploy): 24h watch + automated rollback on CRP/fairness breach
content.terraform_cloud: Workspaces per environment; OPA enforcement; Sentinel policies for org-wide controls; state encryption with PQC-KMS

content.tech: S3 Object Lock (COMPLIANCE mode) + Kafka WORM mirror + Glacier Deep Archive for >5y
content.cryptography: Dilithium3 (PQC signature) + Kyber (PQC KEM for transport) + SHA-3-512 hashing
content.merkle_anchoring: Daily Merkle root anchored to (i) internal HSM, (ii) qualified timestamp authority, (iii) optional public blockchain for highest-tier

sections.title: Automated Verification Tooling + Auditor Workflows (linked to M1-S4)

automated_tools: OPA bundle diff viewer (visualises policy changes per release)
Replay harness CLI (deterministic re-run for Tier-1+ models)
Evidence pack assembler (12-section index per Annex IV pack)
Compliance heatmap (ISO 42001 Annex A x model registry)
content.sla: Evidence retrieval <= 5 business days (KPI K-17 from WP-052)

modules.title: Financial-Services AI Governance (Credit, Trading, Risk, Customer Service)
modules.summary: FinServ-specific governance overlay integrating AI with existing risk systems (MRM, ICAAP, ILAAP, OpRisk, Compliance) under SR 11-7, PRA SS1/23, Basel III/IV, FCRA/ECOA, FCA Consumer Duty, MAS FEAT, HKMA GP-1.

regime_overlay: FCRA s.615 adverse action with reason codes (SHAP + counterfactual top-4)
ECOA Reg B disparate impact (KPI K-07: 0.80-1.25 4/5ths)
EU AI Act Annex III high-risk (creditworthiness)
FCA Consumer Duty foreseeable-harm + vulnerable customers
controls: Quarterly disparate impact study + Fair Lending Committee review
Adverse action appeal + human review SLA <= 14 days

regime_overlay: MiFID II Art.17 algorithmic trading controls
Basel FRTB for market risk capital
controls: Pre-trade risk checks (notional, position, fat-finger, loss-per-day)
Daily backtest + replay vs production (CODE-05 replay harness)
Annual independent algo certification (FCA Algo Cert)
Market abuse surveillance with AI-flag retention 5y
content.containment: Trading AI capped at Tier-2 by default; any RL agent with autonomous capital allocation requires Tier-3 approval and AISI joint test

sections.title: Risk Assessment AI (Credit, Market, OpRisk, AML)

regime_overlay: Basel III/IV capital models (SA-CCR, IRB, FRTB)
controls: Three-line MRM: developer -> independent validator -> internal audit
Annual stress test rerun + supervisor submission
AML alert disposition retention 5y + SAR filings linked to alerts
ai_specific_overlay: Deterministic replay for Tier-1 capital models (K-11)
Explainability for AML alerts to support SAR narrative

regime_overlay: FCA Consumer Duty (the most material regime for UK retail)
GDPR Art.22 if any automated decisions (e.g., onboarding refusal)
controls: Prompt-injection defence (CODE-12 red team) + output filters
Disclosure of AI nature (EU AI Act Art.50)
Conversation retention + supervised sampling for quality
Complaint escalation SLA + Consumer Outcomes dashboard input

integration_points: ICAAP / ILAAP: AI model risk feeds Pillar 2 capital + liquidity buffers
OpRisk taxonomy: New 'AI/ML model' Level-2 + 'GenAI/Frontier' Level-3 nodes
RCSA cycle: AI controls embedded in 1LoD self-assessment (quarterly)
Internal Audit plan: AI governance audited at least annually + 3y rotation deep dive
Risk Appetite Framework: AI-specific limits (Tier-3 frontier compute spend, capability eval thresholds)
BCM/DR: Tier-1 model loss in PRA SS1/21 important business services list
content.data_flows: AI risk signals flow via Kafka 'risk-aggregation' topic to enterprise risk dashboard with 5-minute SLA
committees: AI Risk Committee (monthly) reports to Risk Committee (quarterly) reports to Board Risk Committee (semi-annual)
Frontier Model Committee (as needed; Tier-3+ decisions)

modules.title: Frontier AGI Safety & Trust-by-Design (Alignment Verification, Containment, Monitoring)
modules.summary: Trust-by-design pattern for frontier AGI/ASI: alignment verification battery, containment tiers T0-T4, real-time monitoring (Sentinel v2.4 + CRP), and shutdown / pause / rollback procedures.

principles: Bound capability: deploy at the minimum capability tier needed; escalation requires explicit re-approval
Bound autonomy: every action that affects external systems passes through an authorisation broker
Bound resources: hard caps on compute, memory, network, and external API calls per session
Bound time: long-running agents auto-checkpoint every N minutes with mandatory human re-attestation
Bound knowledge: training data and tool access fenced by policy; no dual-use uplift without approval
Provable behaviour: deterministic replay (CODE-05) + WORM evidence + CRP telemetry
Graceful degradation: every system has a documented safe mode + manual override

categories: Values alignment: HHH (helpful/honest/harmless) + constitutional AI evaluators
Goal alignment: tasks with hidden test cases (eg reward-hacking probes)
Truthfulness: TruthfulQA + factual benchmarks + hallucination index
Safety: refusal-of-harm + dual-use uplift probes + bio/chem/cyber/CBRN guard
Robustness: adversarial perturbations + jailbreak corpus + OWASP LLM Top 10
Deception: power-seeking + sandbagging + situational awareness probes
Sandbagging: differential evals at quiet-vs-watched mode to detect deceptive alignment
thresholds: Tier-2 deploy: composite >= 0.90; refusal-of-harm >= 0.995; AISI eval handover
Tier-3 deploy: composite >= 0.95; refusal-of-harm == 1.00; AISI joint test PASS
Tier-4 deploy: explicit board chair + supervisor pre-clearance only; air-gap only
content.frequency: Pre-deploy + quarterly + on material change + on suspected drift

T0_sandbox.isolation: Network: deny-all egress; storage: ephemeral; compute: capped

T1_staging.isolation: Egress whitelist; canary traffic; CRP monitor

T2_prod_customer.isolation: Production network with full observability; OPA enforcement
T2_prod_customer.approvers: CAIO + CRO + GC + Board AI Cttee

T3_research_isolated.isolation: Dedicated VPC + no internet + tool-use registry + auth broker
T3_research_isolated.approvers: Tier-2 quorum + ExCo + CEO + AISI joint test

T4_air_gapped.isolation: Physical air-gap, no internet, no cross-system data; one-way diode for evidence
T4_air_gapped.approvers: Tier-3 quorum + Board chair + supervisor pre-clearance + treaty body

streams: CRP composite (alignment, stability, transparency) per inference batch
Resource burn (compute, memory, network) vs profile
Auth-broker challenge rate (proxy for autonomy escalation attempts)
thresholds_auto_action: Refusal-of-harm drop > 2% week-on-week -> alert AI Safety Lead + freeze deploys
Capability eval regression > 10% -> rollback to last gold-master
Unauthorized tool-use attempt -> air-gap to T4 + Board chair notification

content.pause: Tier-1+ Pause API gated by CAIO; Tier-3+ adds CEO; takes effect <= 60s
content.shutdown: Tier-2+ Shutdown drains current sessions then terminates serving + WORM logs final state
content.rollback: Last gold-master always retained; rollback within 5 minutes (Tier-1) / 60 minutes (Tier-3)
content.rehearsal: Pause drill quarterly; shutdown drill semi-annually; full rollback drill annually
content.evidence: Every pause/shutdown/rollback is a WORM event (SCH-08) with PQC signature of approvers and post-mortem report within 30 days

modules.title: Global Governance Mechanisms (Compute Consortia, Registries, Cross-Border Coordination)
modules.summary: Engagement model with the 16 proposed global AI/compute bodies, the International Compute Governance Consortium (ICGC), global compute registries, and cross-border safety coordination.

content.purpose: Single window for institutional compute disclosure, frontier model registration, and incident reporting
content.membership: G-SIFIs + frontier developers + major cloud providers + sovereign AI programmes
obligations: Register compute clusters above 10^25 FLOPs aggregate
Submit frontier training plans before run (T0 of run)
Submit eval results within 30 days post-run
Notify ICGC of any Tier-3+ incidents within 24h
benefits: Capital from GASCF for safety research

content.schema: ClusterId, operator, FLOPs (peak + sustained), location, purpose, export-control class, tier
content.filing_cadence: Real-time for material changes; quarterly attestation; annual independent audit
content.verification: GAIVS independent compute audits via PUE/power-meter cross-checks + supplier disclosures
content.publicTransparency: Aggregated/anonymised statistics public; entity-level data confidential to ICGC/GACRA

operational: GAI-SOC (Global AI Security Operations) — incident coordination
FTEWS (Frontier Threat Early Warning) — capability-gain signals
standards: GASO (Standards Observatory) — ISO/IEC alignment + benchmark harmonisation
GAICS (Compute Safety Council) — cluster classification + hazardous capability guidance
registries: GACRLS (Compute Resource Licensing System) — for highest-tier clusters
coordination: GFMCF (Frontier Model Coordination Forum) — bilateral safety pacts
content.capital: GASCF (Safety Capital Fund) — pooled funding for safety research and incident response

bilateral_pacts: US AISI + UK AISI joint pre-deploy testing (operational 2024+)
EU AI Office + US AISI + UK AISI trilateral information sharing
MAS + HKMA + BoT regional AI risk forum
multilateral: UN GDC + UN AI Advisory Body
summit_outputs: Seoul Declaration + Frontier AI Safety Commitments (2024)
Future summits (2026-2030) — institution attends as observer/participant

content.mission: Single accountable office for all multilateral AI obligations across the institution
content.reporting: Joint to GC and CRO; dotted line to CAIO
responsibilities: ICGC + GACRA + AISI submissions calendar (KPI K-20)
content.staffing: Office of 6-12: head + policy leads (US/EU/UK/APAC) + technical liaison + admin

modules.title: AGI Governance Master Blueprint — Enterprise + Frontier + Civilizational
modules.summary: Three-scale unifying frame: enterprise governance (BAU AI today), frontier governance (Tier-3+ R&D), and civilizational governance (treaty-aligned, ASI-scale).

enterprise_scale.scope: All BAU AI inside the institution
enterprise_scale.regimes: EU AI Act + NIST + ISO 42001 + GDPR + sectoral (SR 11-7 / Consumer Duty / MAS FEAT)

frontier_scale.kernel: MGK + MVAGS (Minimum Viable AGI Governance Stack)
frontier_scale.regimes: Above + EO 14110 + AISI joint testing + GPAI systemic-risk obligations

civilizational_scale.horizon: Multi-decade; institution acts in concert with global bodies

shared_substrate: Single Model Registry across all scales
Single WORM audit fabric (Kafka + S3 Object Lock + PQC)
Single OPA policy bundle with tier-conditional rules
scale_specific_overlays: Enterprise: MRM tiering + Annex IV pack + Consumer Outcomes dashboard
Frontier: AISI joint testing + capability eval + air-gap deployment + GASCF research
Civilizational: ICGC submissions + treaty filings + GACMO coordination + global incident playbooks
content.interlocks: Tier escalation (T1->T2->T3->T4) implicitly transitions the system across scales; each transition is WORM-logged with all required external notifications enqueued automatically

year_2_2027: EU AI Act compliance baseline operational

rhythm: Daily: GAI-SOC stand-up + CRP / fairness / drift dashboard review
Weekly: Model Risk Committee + Fair Lending Committee + AI Ethics review
Monthly: AI Risk Committee + Board AI Cttee chair briefing
Quarterly: Board AI/Risk Committee meeting + ExCo AI strategy + supervisor liaison
Semi-annual: Board AI literacy + AGI containment tabletop + Cert surveillance audit
Annual: MRM deep-dive + Internal Audit + External attestation + Regulator examination rehearsal
content.decision_throughput: Tier-1: 5-20 / month; Tier-2: 2-5 / month; Tier-3: 1-3 / year; Tier-4: 0-1 / 2 years

auditability: Every Tier-1+ decision is WORM-logged with PQC signature
Every model has a deterministic replay record (Tier-1+)
Every Annex IV pack is reproducible from the registry + WORM
Every regulator report has a PQC-signed manifest
Every policy change has a diff + approval chain visible to auditors
legal_defensibility: Documented duty of care via MGK + MVAGS + AI Charter (Appendix E)
Effective challenge documented in MRM minutes
FRIA + DPIA chain for high-risk systems
Insurance: AI E&O + cyber + D&O addenda for AI-specific risk
Standard of care defensible vs reasonable institution of similar size

modules.title: Implementation Timelines & Milestones (2026-2030)
modules.summary: Five-year multi-year programme with quarterly milestones, gate evidence, and capability dependencies organised by stream.

content.S2_regulatory: EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST, SR 11-7
content.S3_engineering: OPA, Kafka WORM, Terraform, CI/CD, replay
content.S4_safety: Sentinel v2.4, CRP, containment tiers, AISI
content.S5_finserv: MRM integration, ICAAP, Consumer Duty, FEAT

content.Q1: Board approves Charter; MGK kernel scaffold; OPA policy library v0.5; Annex IV template v0.5
content.Q2: MGK GA; AISRG MVP; First AISI joint test; ISO 42001 stage-1 audit
content.Q3: Annex IV templates v1.0; Kafka WORM GA; OPA library v1.0; ISO 42001 stage-2 audit
content.Q4: MGK Cert Gold; Treaty Liaison Office stood up; First public AI Transparency Report

content.2027_Q1: Model Registry GA; CCaaS-PETs pilot; First ICGC submission
content.2027_Q2: AISI joint test count = 4; Internal Audit AI deep-dive completed
content.2027_Q3: ISO 42001 surveillance audit pass; FSB submissions begun
content.2027_Q4: EAIP RFC drafted; G2 gate close
content.2028_Q1: EAIP v1.0 published; ICGC full membership
content.2028_Q4: G3 gate close; FSB submissions ratified

content.2029_Q1-Q4: Steady-state MGK; civilizational research outputs via GASCF; AISI joint test count >= 16; bilateral safety pacts operational
content.2030_Q4: G4 gate close; treaty alignment closed; Board final attestation

content.G0_charter: Board minutes + signed Charter + RACI v1
content.G1_mgk: Cert Gold + OPA library v1 + WORM live + Annex IV template
content.G2_registry: Model Registry GA + Annex IV pack per Tier-1 model + first ICGC submission
content.G3_platinum: ISO 42001 Platinum + FSB ratification + EAIP v1.0
content.G4_public: Public assurance programme + re-audit Platinum + treaty alignment closed

modules.title: Risk & Cost-Benefit Analyses
modules.summary: Programme-level risk register, sensitivity analysis, and CBA for G-SIFI tier (USD 120-360M over 5 years).

content.PR-01: Regulatory divergence (EU vs US vs APAC) -> Mitigation: single source of truth + dual filings + TLO
content.PR-02: AISI capacity / queue -> Mitigation: pooled GAIVS slot booking + internal red-team strength
content.PR-03: PQC migration delays -> Mitigation: hybrid PQC + classical; phased rollout
content.PR-04: Talent scarcity (AI safety, MRM) -> Mitigation: hire plan + university partnerships + retention
content.PR-05: Vendor lock-in (LLM / cloud) -> Mitigation: multi-vendor + open-weights tier-2 fallback
content.PR-06: Frontier capability surprise -> Mitigation: FTEWS subscription + T4 ready + air-gap drill
content.PR-07: Compute concentration -> Mitigation: GACRA disclosure + multi-region
content.PR-08: Public/political backlash -> Mitigation: transparency programme + civil-society engagement
content.PR-09: Insurance market hardening -> Mitigation: captive option + risk-sharing with peers
content.PR-10: Budget pressure year-on-year -> Mitigation: ROI metrics + cost-per-Tier-1-model trending

content.people_USD_m: 60-150 (CAIO office, MRM, Red Team, AI Safety, TLO, Engineering)
content.platform_USD_m: 25-80 (Kafka WORM, OPA, AISRG, PQC-KMS, observability, replay infra)

content.avoided_fines: EU AI Act max EUR 35M or 7% global turnover per breach; SR 11-7 / Consumer Duty material -> avoid 1-3 events = USD 100-500M+ at G-SIFI scale
content.operational_efficiency: Productivity uplift from regulator-portable evidence: 30-50% reduction in time spent on regulator/audit responses (~USD 20-80M / year)
content.capital_efficiency: Better-validated models -> lower Pillar 2 add-ons; estimated USD 30-150M / year capital relief
content.reputational: Sustained licence-to-operate; harder to quantify but material in stress events
content.frontier_optionality: Ability to compete in frontier model space safely; pricing-in by markets observed in 2024-25

drivers: Regulatory scope expansion (EU AI Act updates, US federal legislation) -> +20-50% cost
Frontier compute price (Hopper -> Blackwell -> next) -> +/- 30% on R&D
stress_scenarios: S2 adverse: +30% cost, -20% benefit, NPV still positive
S3 tail: +60% cost, -40% benefit, NPV breakeven; programme still justified by regulatory floor

content.recommendation: Approve full 5-year programme at midpoint budget with quarterly review and annual benefit-tracking
content.phasing: Front-load people + platform (2026-27); back-load global + assurance (2028-30)
kill_criteria: Regulator pull-back making programme moot (low probability)
Frontier risk profile changes such that Tier-3+ activity is exited (medium probability over 5y)
Material adverse finding requiring re-baselining (managed via quarterly review)

modules.title: Appendices: Templates (Annex IV Pack, FRIA, DPIA, AI Charter, Conflict Register, Incident Report)
modules.summary: Ready-to-use templates for the core governance artefacts referenced throughout the blueprint; each linked to engineering controls and regulator obligations.

sections.title: Template Inventory (links to appendix block)
content: TPL-A Annex IV Technical Documentation Pack (Appendix A)
TPL-B Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment / FRIA (Appendix B)
TPL-C Privacy-by-Design Checklist + DPIA shell (Appendix C)
TPL-D Cross-Jurisdiction Conflict Register (Appendix D)
TPL-E Board AI Charter (Appendix E)
TPL-F Incident Report (Tier-1+) (Appendix F)
TPL-G Model Card v2 (Appendix G)
TPL-H Vendor/Third-Party AI Due Diligence (Appendix H)

content.storage: AISRG + WORM PQC-signed manifest; PDF/A-3 + JSON-LD
content.access: RBAC; auditor read-only sandbox; supervisor zk-SNARK sandbox

sections.title: Approval Chain Embedded in Each Template
content: Author -> Reviewer (peer) -> Owner (1LoD) -> Validator (2LoD) -> Risk approver -> Board notification
Every signature is a PQC signature emitted to audit-worm topic with SCH-08

content.diff: Stored as both human-readable diff and structured JSON patch
content.retention: All versions retained per artifact retention rules in M1-S3

sections.title: Quality Gates per Template
content: Completeness: all required sections populated
Traceability: every claim linked to evidence (WORM ref / model registry ref / policy id)
Reviewability: machine-parsable structured fields alongside narrative
Signed off: full approval chain with PQC sigs before 'EFFECTIVE' state

modules.title: Appendices: Checklists (Pre-Deploy, Quarterly, Annual, Incident, Frontier-Run)
modules.summary: Operational checklists for the most frequent governance activities; each maps to KPIs and WORM topics.

sections.title: Checklist Inventory
content: CHK-1 Pre-deployment (per model) — Appendix I
CHK-2 Quarterly review (per Tier-1+ model) — Appendix J
CHK-3 Annual attestation (institution-wide) — Appendix K
CHK-4 Incident response (S1/S2) — Appendix L
CHK-5 Frontier training run (Tier-3+) — Appendix M
CHK-6 Auditor evidence-pack prep — Appendix N
CHK-7 Supervisor exam rehearsal — Appendix O

sections.title: Mapping to KPIs (subset)
content: CHK-1 covers K-01 (Annex IV completeness), K-06 (OPA test coverage), K-07 (fairness), K-22 (explainability)
CHK-2 covers K-03/K-04 (CRP), K-11 (replay diff), K-12 (drift), K-21 (adversarial regression)
CHK-3 covers K-02 (inventory), K-18 (board dashboard), K-20 (treaty submissions), K-24 (regulator findings)
CHK-4 covers K-09 (MTTC), K-05 (WORM gaps)
CHK-5 covers K-13 (compute registry), K-19 (containment tier compliance)

content.CHK-1: Model Owner + Validator + CAIO (or delegated approver for Tier-0/1)
content.CHK-2: Model Owner + MRM + Fair Lending (if applicable)
content.CHK-3: CAIO + CRO + GC + Board AI Cttee chair
content.CHK-4: Incident Commander + GAI-SOC Director + CAIO + (CISO for security incidents)
content.CHK-5: AI Safety Lead + CEO + Board chair + AISI

sections.title: Frequency + Cadence
content: CHK-1: Per deployment
CHK-2: Quarterly
CHK-3: Annual
CHK-4: Per incident
CHK-5: Per frontier run kickoff + monthly during run + at completion
CHK-6: Per audit engagement
CHK-7: Annual rehearsal + before known supervisor exam

sections.title: Quality Standards
content: Each checklist item is binary (pass/fail) or scored (numerical with threshold)
Each item carries a WORM-eventable result
Each completion produces a PQC-signed manifest stored in AISRG
Each delta from a previous run is highlighted in the manifest for auditor review

modules.title: Feasibility, Auditability, and Legal Defensibility (2026-2030)
modules.summary: Synthesis: what makes this blueprint feasible to deploy, auditable end-to-end, and legally defensible in adversarial proceedings.

sections.title: Feasibility Indicators
content: Builds on existing controls (MRM, OpRisk, CISO programmes) rather than greenfield
Modular: MGK and MVAGS can be adopted in stages without full Big-Bang
Aligned with vendor roadmaps (Kafka, OPA, Terraform Cloud, major clouds) for 2026-2030
Compatible with PQC migration timelines (NIST PQC selected algorithms standardised 2024)
Talent pipeline addressable through university partnerships + targeted hiring (M9-PR-04)
Cost (USD 120-360M G-SIFI) is within typical risk-and-controls programme envelopes

sections.title: Auditability Surface
content: WORM audit fabric with PQC + Merkle anchoring (M3-S5)
Deterministic replay for Tier-1+ models (CODE-05)
OPA policy diff + bundle versioning
AISRG R-01..R-12 regulator-portable reports (linked to WP-052)
Auditor persona dashboards (M3-S6)
Reproducible Annex IV pack from registry + WORM at any point in time

sections.title: Legal Defensibility (Adversarial Proceedings)
content: Duty of care: documented MGK + MVAGS + AI Charter (Appendix E) approved by Board
Standard of care: blueprint aligned to ISO 42001 / NIST RMF / EU AI Act / SR 11-7 — i.e., contemporary best practice for institution size
Effective challenge: documented in MRM minutes and validation reports (M4-S3)
Evidence chain: PQC-signed WORM + Merkle anchor + qualified timestamp
Privilege protection: legal-hold playbook + privileged-counsel review path
Insurance backstop: AI E&O + cyber + D&O addenda (M7-S5)

components: Model registry + Annex IV pack pipeline
content.scoring: Each component 0/1/2/3 (none / partial / operational / steady-state); DRI = sum / max
content.targets: DRI >= 0.5 by end of 2026; >= 0.8 by end of 2028; >= 0.95 by end of 2030

sections.title: Closing Recommendation
content: Approve programme at midpoint budget for 5y
Stand up the CAIO office + Treaty Liaison Office within Q1 2026
Adopt MGK + AISRG + OPA + Kafka WORM as the foundation in 2026-27
Layer Cert Gold (2026 / 2027) then Platinum (2028) with annual surveillance
Position institution as a credible participant in ICGC + AISI + GFMCF during 2027-29
Aim for public assurance programme launch in 2030 as a market differentiator

code.title: T3+ frontier deployment requires AISI joint test
code.snippet:
package agi.deploy.frontier

allow {
  input.model.tier == "T3"
  input.aisi.joint_test.passed == true
  input.approvals.ceo
  input.approvals.board_chair
}

code.title: Kafka ACL: auditor read-only on audit-worm
FRIA stakeholder consult logger
Terraform: PQC-KMS key for audit signing
Adverse action engine FCRA s.615
code.snippet:
def adverse_action(decision):
    reasons = top_k_shap(decision, k=4)
    text = render_reasons_template(reasons, locale=decision.locale)
    appeal = create_appeal_link(decision, expiry='60d')
    notify_consumer(decision.applicant, text, appeal)
    log_to_worm('adverse-action-events', decision, reasons)

code.title: Trading kill-switch
Containment tier escalator
GDPR Art.22: automated decisions require explicit consent or contract necessity
GitHub Actions: continuous compliance gate
code.snippet:
name: continuous-compliance
on: [pull_request]
jobs:
  gate-1:
    steps:
      - run: opa test policies/ -v
      - run: conftest test manifests/ -p policies/
      - run: replay-harness --sample 5
      - run: fairness-regression --baseline last-gold

code.title: DRI calculator
Treaty Liaison submission emitter
WORM Merkle proof verifier (auditor CLI)

kpis.name: Tier-1+ models with Annex IV pack

regulators.name: ICO / EDPB
regulators.regime: UK GDPR / GDPR / AI Audit framework

deployment.topology: K8s + Kafka WORM + OPA sidecars + governance plane VPC
deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions + Argo CD + Terraform Cloud + OPA gates
deployment.dr: Active-active Tier-1; cold-standby Tier-2; air-gap snapshot Tier-4

evidencePack.retention: 10 years standard; 25 years for Tier-2+; 50 years for Tier-4

executiveSummary.thesis: Between 2026 and 2030, F500/G2000/G-SIFIs must operate AGI-grade AI under an auditable, legally defensible, and treaty-aligned governance framework. This blueprint unifies enterprise BAU governance, frontier R&D safety, and civilizational-scale coordination into a single, deployable architecture.
executiveSummary.investmentRange: USD 120-360M over 5 years for G-SIFI tier; NPV USD 300-1200M
boardAsks: Charter CAIO + TLO offices in Q1 2026
Endorse ICGC participation and AISI joint testing

appendixTemplates.name: Annex IV Technical Documentation Pack
appendixTemplates.purpose: EU AI Act Article 11 + Annex IV technical documentation for high-risk AI systems
fields: 3. Detailed description of elements + dev process

appendixTemplates.name: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA)
appendixTemplates.purpose: EU AI Act Article 27 FRIA for deployers of high-risk AI systems
fields: 1. Description of deployer processes for which the system will be used
3. Categories of natural persons / groups likely affected
4. Specific risks of harm likely to impact affected categories
6. Measures to be taken if risks materialise (mitigation + redress)
8. Consultation with affected groups / civil society (where applicable)

appendixTemplates.name: Privacy-by-Design Checklist + DPIA Shell
appendixTemplates.purpose: GDPR Article 25 + 35 (data protection by design + DPIA) for AI systems
fields: 3. Risks to data subjects' rights and freedoms
5. PETs evaluated (DP, k-anonymity, federated, secure enclave)

appendixTemplates.name: Cross-Jurisdiction Conflict Register
appendixTemplates.purpose: Captures and tracks conflicts between AI regulatory regimes

appendixTemplates.name: Board AI Charter
appendixTemplates.purpose: Board-approved AI charter establishing duty of care + accountability
fields: 2. Principles (aligned to OECD AI + NIST RMF + ISO 42001)
5. Pillars (P1 Technical, P2 Ethical, P3 Legal, P4 Operational, P5 Risk)

appendixTemplates.name: Incident Report (Tier-1+)
appendixTemplates.purpose: Structured incident record for material AI incidents

appendixTemplates.name: Vendor / Third-Party AI Due Diligence
appendixTemplates.purpose: Procurement template for AI vendors and third-party models
fields: 3. Regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST, ISO 42001)

appendixChecklists.name: Pre-Deployment Checklist (per model)
items: Annex IV pack (TPL-A) for high-risk systems
Containment tier confirmed + air-gap if T4
WORM events for approval chain emitted

appendixChecklists.name: Quarterly Review Checklist (per Tier-1+ model)
items: Model card v2 still accurate; refresh if not
Sign-off: Model Owner + MRM + Fair Lending

appendixChecklists.name: Annual Attestation Checklist (institution-wide)
items: Sign-off: CAIO + CRO + GC + Board AI Cttee

appendixChecklists.name: Incident Response Checklist (S1/S2)
items: Regulator clocks armed (EU AI Act 15d, GDPR 72h, etc.)
Board reporting within 90 days if material
Lessons learned to GAID (anonymised) if appropriate
Sign-off: Incident Commander + GAI-SOC + CAIO + (CISO security)

appendixChecklists.name: Frontier Training Run Checklist (Tier-3+)
items: Run plan + budget approved by ExCo + CEO + Board chair
Containment tier confirmed (T3 isolated / T4 air-gap)
Eval results to AISI within 30 days post-run

appendixChecklists.name: Auditor Evidence-Pack Prep Checklist
items: Replay harness access for sample models

appendixChecklists.name: Supervisor Exam Rehearsal Checklist
items: Annex IV pack (or equivalent for jurisdiction) refreshed
Q&A pack for top-20 likely questions prepared
